title: Causal stint-state features
summary: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models latent tyre pace with pit-stop state resets and time-varying degradation.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models latent tyre pace with pit-stop state resets and time-varying degradation. Current features use mostly same-row ratios and static group medians; ordered within-driver/race rolling features may capture the accumulating hazard before a stop.
implementation_hint: Sort by Race, Driver, LapNumber. Add lagged/rolling-only-past features: previous lap time delta, rolling 3-lap pace slope, rolling degradation slope, stint min/max/mean pace so far, laps since last PitStop, and change from previous lap within stint. Avoid centered or future-looking windows.
expected_effect: Better discrimination of imminent pit stops versus merely old tyres, especially when degradation accelerates late in a stint.
risk: If row order or Race names mix seasons badly, rolling features can leak or become noisy; validate with race-group or year-aware folds, not only random split.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
